definition: Trade short-term income for skill equity in early career; skill ROI compounds 100x+ higher than salary growth.
rationale: Coherent framework on early-career capital allocation (skill vs. income vs. savings). All members sit on distinct axes of the same decision tree.

definition: Compensation scales with perceived risk assumed; insurance/warranties let you sell risk itself as product.
rationale: Load-bearing axis: low-risk W-2 → moderate-risk skill monetization → high-risk risk-transfer markets. Members are distinct price tiers, not redundant.

definition: Defer facility/non-urgent work; maximize sales/marketing to generate cash for capital reinvestment.
rationale: Original name 'Constraint Management' too generic. Members show a specific pattern: cap-constrained businesses must grow revenue first, defer capex. Eisenhower Matrix member dropped as generic framework, not specific claim.

definition: Change environment and reference group to shift behavior faster than time/willpower alone.
rationale: Only 2 members, one incomplete. Core claim merges cleanly into 177 as environmental/contextual factor for learning success. Remove malformed reference-group claim.

definition: AI automation + hacker-level ops enable solo founders to scale to millions in revenue with zero headcount.
rationale: Tight cluster on AI-as-force-multiplier for solo operations. All members quantified (120k tickets, $150k cost, millions revenue/FTE). Load-bearing claim about new business model.

definition: Attract via free/low-friction entry; position paid tiers as 10x more valuable via decoy/anchoring; reinvest in quality after product-market fit.
rationale: Centroid 'Solo Business Scaling' is vague. These 7 members all describe pricing architecture: free funnel → paid positioning → quality tier. Rename captures pattern.

definition: Highest leadership job is setting and enforcing quality standards; standards separate winners from failures.
rationale: Load-bearing claim about leadership lever. All 4 members address standards-as-mechanism for culture/quality. Coherent and specific.

definition: Separate one-time skill-building (high-value) from recurring consumables (low-value) in pricing; prevents churn from misaligned expectations.
rationale: Original 'Churn Reduction' too broad. Members show specific pattern: skill/training != subscription; pricing them together kills retention. Rename sharpens the claim.

definition: Behavioral intensity post-breakpoint; ranges from tiny reactions (high fortitude) to life-disrupting responses (low fortitude).
rationale: Specialized psychological dimension with 6 members providing examples + theory. Load-bearing for resilience/antifragility framework. Niche but valid.

definition: Procedural (doing) knowledge replaces declarative (knowing); reading/watching without implementation teaches nothing.
rationale: Core axis: doing != knowing. Load-bearing for training/education design. Contrasts with video-as-learning myth. 3 tight members.

definition: Personal savings rate > absolute income for QoL improvements; don't increase lifestyle proportionally to income growth.
rationale: Distinct from 177 (skill investing). This is personal capital allocation post-income. 4 members on savings-discipline axis. Coherent lifestyle economics frame.

definition: Hire to pull (want them), not push (escape pain); reserve equity for future A-players; ruthlessly filter from 10 applicants.
rationale: Coherent hiring philosophy across stages. 4 members address selection intensity + forward-allocation strategy. Load-bearing for scaled hiring.

canonical_name: Warm vs. Cold Sales Process Mismatch
aliases: Sales Motion Changes With Cold Traffic
definition: One process fits both (cold=complex); warm process fails on cold traffic; warm leads suffer from skipped rigor (lower close rates).
rationale: Load-bearing failure mode: applying warm sales playbook to cold traffic or vice versa kills conversion. 5 members all illustrate this mismatch.

definition: Don't need to operate your business perfectly or at scale to teach others; only working advice matters.
rationale: Centroid 'Teaching Authority' vague. Rename sharpens: you teach advice-that-works, not your own operation. Second member (operator recruitment) is sub-application, kept as alias.

definition: Reframe value pitch from unbelievable savings to believable margin arbitrage; 20% guaranteed cost floor forces 50%+ upside threshold.
rationale: Members all address credibility layer: 'Save $200k' sounds like scam; 'Give you 20% margin' sounds true. 3 members on value articulation specificity.
